summary: Well‑recorded show with strong first half and a smoking Playin > Uncle John’s > Playin sequence, plus solid Garcia and Brent performances
review: The concert is praised for its tight playing and especially the bright, tropical feel of the Playin > Uncle John’s > Playin jam that opens set two. Reviewers note Jerry’s guitar was “definitely on” and Brent’s keyboards were in full voice, while the recording is described as clear and well‑captured, making it an enjoyable listen.

ai_rating.rationale: All six reviews are 4–5 stars, consistently highlight strong musical performance and a good recording; no major complaints.

recording_quality.technical_notes: One reviewer calls it “really well‑recorded”; no reports of dropouts or muffiness, suggesting clear and balanced sound despite unknown source.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong first set with highlights like Hell In A Bucket and Sugaree, followed by an especially vibrant second‑set opener (Playin > Uncle John’s > Playin) that keeps the momentum high.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia is noted as “definitely on” with solid guitar tone and vocals.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboard/vocals) described as being in full throat, adding energy to the performance.
